claims.claim: Cabinet banned all government and GLC Hari Raya open houses and restricted ministers' overseas travel as cost-saving.
claims.source: Prime Minister's Office verbatim transcript, 11 March 2026, https://www.pmo.gov.my/en/verbatim-en/teks-verbatim-yab-perdana-menteri-sidang-media-11-mac-2026/
claims.correction: Wording should specify government agencies, GLCs and GLICs, plus ministers, administration members and departments restricted from overseas visits except scheduled or compulsory engagements.
claims.stale_check: No stale issue found; this was announced on 11 March 2026.

claims.claim: Government cancels all Raya open houses for fiscal discipline.
claims.source: Prime Minister's Office verbatim transcript, 11 March 2026, https://www.pmo.gov.my/en/verbatim-en/teks-verbatim-yab-perdana-menteri-sidang-media-11-mac-2026/
claims.correction: The order covered government agencies, GLCs and GLICs, not all Raya open houses in Malaysia. Private, household and some state-level events were not necessarily covered.

claims.claim: The savings fund 1.8 minutes of the daily fuel subsidy.
claims.source: Ministry of Finance press citation, 26 March 2026, https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/en/news/press-citations/subsidies-under-ron95-budi-diesel-hit-rm4-bln-monthly-mof; Business Today citing DPMM, 17 March 2026, https://www.businesstoday.com.my/2026/03/17/raya-open-house-cancellation-may-cost-businesses-up-to-rm25-million/
claims.correction: At RM4 billion per month, the subsidy is about RM133.3 million per day, or about RM92,600 per minute. If cancelled open-house revenue was RM15 million to RM25 million, that equals roughly 162 to 270 minutes of one day's subsidy, not 1.8 minutes. If the intended savings figure was about RM167,000, no primary source was found for it.
claims.stale_check: The RM4 billion figure was current on 26 March 2026; by 15 April 2026 MOF reported RON95 subsidies around RM4 billion monthly and diesel around RM3 billion monthly, so total fuel subsidy burden may have changed.

claims.claim: The savings are a fraction of the RM4B monthly fuel subsidy.
claims.source: Ministry of Finance press citation, 26 March 2026, https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/en/news/press-citations/subsidies-under-ron95-budi-diesel-hit-rm4-bln-monthly-mof; Ministry of Finance press citation, 15 April 2026, https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/en/news/press-citations/budi95-price-remains-unsubsidised-ron95-ron97-down-25-sen-diesel-drops-75-sen
claims.correction: MOF said petrol and diesel subsidies were estimated at up to RM4 billion monthly on 26 March 2026. On 15 April 2026, MOF reported RON95 alone at about RM4 billion monthly and diesel at about RM3 billion monthly. The analysis should date the RM4 billion figure and clarify which subsidy category it refers to.
claims.stale_check: Potentially stale: later MOF reporting on 15 April 2026 gave a higher combined fuel subsidy burden.

claims.claim: Open houses are Malaysia's most cross-racial political tradition.
claims.correction: Primary sources support that Malaysia Open House events are intended to foster unity and understanding among multi-racial communities, but I found no primary or peer-reviewed source proving they are Malaysia's 'most' cross-racial political tradition.

claims.claim: Open houses are a taxpayer-funded patronage vehicle.
claims.source: Dewan Rakyat reporting via Astro Awani, 24 September 2013, https://international.astroawani.com/malaysia-news/government-spent-rm288-million-pms-raya-open-house-22749
claims.correction: Taxpayer funding for at least some official open houses is documented, including RM2.88 million for the 2013 Prime Minister and Cabinet Hari Raya open house. The stronger claim that the institution is a 'patronage vehicle' requires procurement, contracting, or political-benefit evidence not found in primary sources.

claims.claim: A Malay MP hosting diverse guests is real muhibbah, and contract signalling.
claims.correction: The muhibbah/unity aspect is broadly supported for official open-house events. The 'contract signalling' part was not verified from a primary source.

claims.claim: Caterers and tent firms book RM15-25M.
claims.source: Business Today citing Dewan Perniagaan Melayu Malaysia executive council member Mohd Dzahir Jamudin, 17 March 2026, https://www.businesstoday.com.my/2026/03/17/raya-open-house-cancellation-may-cost-businesses-up-to-rm25-million/
claims.correction: The cited estimate was that the private sector could lose RM15 million to RM25 million in expected revenue across ministries and agencies. It was not limited to caterers and tent firms; it included event management, florists, gift suppliers, merchandise, printing, logistics and subcontractors.

claims.claim: Female caterers and women-led food SMEs lose real income.
claims.source: Department of Statistics Malaysia, Economic Census 2023 Women-Owned Statistics, 30 April 2025, https://statistics.gov.my/portal-main/release-content/economic-census--women-owned-statistics; Business Today citing DPMM, 17 March 2026, https://www.businesstoday.com.my/2026/03/17/raya-open-house-cancellation-may-cost-businesses-up-to-rm25-million/
claims.correction: It is plausible that some women-owned F&B businesses lost income, and DOSM records women-owned establishments in F&B. However, no primary source verified that female caterers or women-led food SMEs were specifically or disproportionately affected by this cancellation.

claims.claim: Rural constituents lose their one annual face-to-face access to their YB.
claims.correction: This should be reframed as an argument or anecdotal concern unless supported by constituency-level evidence that open houses are the only annual face-to-face access point for rural constituents.

claims.source: Business Today citing Dewan Perniagaan Melayu Malaysia executive council member Mohd Dzahir Jamudin, 17 March 2026, https://www.businesstoday.com.my/2026/03/17/raya-open-house-cancellation-may-cost-businesses-up-to-rm25-million/
claims.correction: The estimate comes from DPMM as reported by Business Today, not from a government audit or procurement dataset. Attribute it clearly.

claims.source: Department of Statistics Malaysia, Food and Beverage Services report, Economic Census 2016, https://economiccensus.dosm.gov.my/ec2/images/2023/PUBLICATION/BE%202016/Penerbitan%20banci%20Ekonomi%202016%20Makanan%20dan%20Minuman.pdf; Department of Statistics Malaysia, Economic Census 2023 Women-Owned Statistics, 30 April 2025, https://statistics.gov.my/portal-main/release-content/economic-census--women-owned-statistics
claims.correction: DOSM documents women-owned establishments in F&B, including event catering, but I found no primary source showing catering is female-dominated in ownership or employment.

claims.claim: The kampung auntie loses her one day of direct access; T20 networkers lose only a convenience.
claims.correction: This is rhetorical class framing, not verified fact. It needs survey, ethnographic, or constituency-service evidence.

claims.claim: A simpler open house (smaller tent, potluck) could have preserved the tradition at near-zero cost.
claims.correction: No source verified feasibility, legality, security requirements, procurement implications, or actual cost of a potluck or smaller-tent official event. 'Near-zero cost' is an overclaim.

claims.claim: The government chose a total ban.
claims.source: Prime Minister's Office verbatim transcript, 11 March 2026, https://www.pmo.gov.my/en/verbatim-en/teks-verbatim-yab-perdana-menteri-sidang-media-11-mac-2026/
claims.correction: The government chose a total ban for government agencies, GLCs and GLICs. It was not a total ban on all open houses by all actors in Malaysia.

claims.claim: Other Muslim states scaled down festive hospitality rather than eliminating it.
claims.source: Indonesia State Secretariat, 2026 Eid open house, https://www.setneg.go.id/baca/index/istana_terbuka_untuk_rakyat_presiden_prabowo_prioritaskan_masyarakat_pada_gelar_griya_idulfitri; Indonesia Cabinet Secretariat, 2026 Eid open house, https://setkab.go.id/en/president-prabowo-opens-palace-to-public-for-eid-al-fitr-open-house/
claims.correction: Indonesia is a verified example: officials were urged to lessen or avoid excessive open-house and halal bihalal gatherings while the President still held an open house. The plural 'states' needs more than one verified example; I verified one country.

claims.claim: Malaysia's all-or-nothing approach signals optics, not optimisation.
claims.source: Prime Minister's Office verbatim transcript, 11 March 2026, https://www.pmo.gov.my/en/verbatim-en/teks-verbatim-yab-perdana-menteri-sidang-media-11-mac-2026/
claims.correction: The PMO transcript supports a spending-moderation rationale. The claim about optics versus optimisation is interpretation and should be labelled as analysis unless backed by budget alternatives or Cabinet papers.

claims.claim: Every party plays this in power and mourns it out.
claims.correction: This broad political generalisation needs evidence across parties and administrations. No primary source was found verifying it.

claims.claim: The ban returns when oil drops or an election nears.
claims.correction: This is a prediction, not a verifiable factual claim. It should be presented as speculation or removed.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Government cancels all Raya open houses for fiscal discipline.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The official decision covered government agencies, GLCs and GLICs, not all Raya open houses in Malaysia.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: Prime Minister's Office verbatim transcript, 11 March 2026, https://www.pmo.gov.my/en/verbatim-en/teks-verbatim-yab-perdana-menteri-sidang-media-11-mac-2026/

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: The savings fund 1.8 minutes of the daily fuel subsidy.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The arithmetic does not match the cited RM4 billion monthly subsidy and RM15 million to RM25 million estimated revenue impact. RM15 million to RM25 million equals roughly 162 to 270 minutes of a daily subsidy calculated from RM4 billion monthly.
DOSM verifies women-owned establishments in F&B but not that catering is female-dominated.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: Department of Statistics Malaysia, Economic Census 2023 Women-Owned Statistics, 30 April 2025, https://statistics.gov.my/portal-main/release-content/economic-census--women-owned-statistics

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Caterers and tent firms book RM15-25M.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The cited estimate was private-sector expected revenue loss across multiple supplier categories, not only caterers and tent firms.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Rural constituents lose their one annual face-to-face access to their YB.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: No primary evidence was found that open houses are the only annual face-to-face access point for rural constituents.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: A simpler open house (smaller tent, potluck) could have preserved the tradition at near-zero cost.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: No source verified near-zero cost or operational feasibility for official government or GLC events.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Every party plays this in power and mourns it out.
cardinal_sin_flags.why: No primary evidence was supplied or found for this all-party historical claim.

omitted_facts: MOF later reported on 15 April 2026 that RON95 subsidies had risen to about RM4 billion monthly and diesel subsidies to about RM3 billion monthly, which means the analysis's RM4 billion monthly fuel-subsidy comparison may be outdated or category-confused: https://www.mof.gov.my/portal/en/news/press-citations/budi95-price-remains-unsubsidised-ron95-ron97-down-25-sen-diesel-drops-75-sen

PMO's 11 March 2026 transcript frames the cancellation as a response to West Asia conflict risks, oil and gas supply concerns, and fiscal caution, not only generic fiscal discipline: https://www.pmo.gov.my/en/verbatim-en/teks-verbatim-yab-perdana-menteri-sidang-media-11-mac-2026/

Tourism Malaysia says Malaysia Open House has been organised since 2001 as a national tourism activity to foster unity, understanding, and closer relations between leaders and people: https://www.tourism.gov.my/news/megafam/view/deepavali-open-house-mrtm

Indonesia provides a relevant comparator: its government urged officials and ministries to lessen or avoid excessive Eid open-house gatherings, while the President still held an open palace event for the public: https://setkab.go.id/en/president-prabowo-opens-palace-to-public-for-eid-al-fitr-open-house/
source_assessment: The original analysis cites only two prior T4A issues, creating circular sourcing and no independent source diversity. It does not cite PMO, MOF, Tourism Malaysia, DOSM, parliamentary records, business associations, vendors, opposition figures, state governments, procurement records, or affected community voices. Political, social and economic claims are presented with little primary-source support. Source diversity is therefore extremely weak.
overall_assessment: The core announcement is verified: the Cabinet decided that government agencies, GLCs and GLICs would not organise Aidilfitri open houses, and overseas travel by ministers and administration members would be restricted. However, many surrounding claims are either rhetorical, overbroad, arithmetically wrong, or unsupported by primary sources. The most serious factual problem is the '1.8 minutes' subsidy calculation, which does not reconcile with the RM4 billion monthly subsidy figure or the RM15 million to RM25 million estimated supplier impact. The piece is usable only after tightening scope, dating subsidy figures, removing unsupported social claims, and separating verified facts from interpretation.
